course_title: Law in the Criminal Justice System

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the structure and origin of the Australian legal system.
Appreciate the importance of fundamental legal principles and political structures and how they apply to criminal justice system institutions and processes.
Analyse and critically evaluate different theoretical approaches underpinning legal principles and their application to the criminal justice system.
Apply skills required to engage in effective legal research and analysis of case law, legislation, and secondary sources.
Apply skills required to communicate effectively within a legal and policy context.

assessments.task_description: During the Week 6 Seminar, you will be expected to complete and submit the In Class Assessment. This Assessment Activity is designed to demonstrate your engagement with and understanding of content covered in weeks 1 - 5 of the course. To prepare for this Activity, you will be guided to engage with materials and discussions during the Week 1 - 5 seminars, and you may be prompted to conduct some additional research, activities, and/or readings on relevant topics throughout these weeks in order to complete the assessment. A detailed task guide and marking criteria will be available in the Assessment folder on the course Blackboard site in Week 1.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The In Class Assessment must be submitted electronically via the submission link on the course Blackboard site. Instructions for submission will be provided in the task guidelines.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The end-of-semester exam comprises two problem-based questions. This assessment task builds on the skills developed over the course of the semester and applies these skills to the content covered in the second block. Instructions and marking criteria will be made available in Week 10.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Seminars

The seminar topics are split into two blocks. The first block focuses on developing students’ foundational knowledge of Australian legal institutions, law-making power, legal principles, and rights protections, and on enhancing students’ legal literacy skills. The block concludes with an in-class assessment in Week 6. Seminar topics include: 

1. Why Should Criminologists Understand Law and Legal Systems?

2. Legal Institutions and Key Legal Principles

3. Justice, Fairness and Human Rights

4. Sources of Law – How to Read Case Law and Legislation 

5. Engaging with Criminal Law and Procedure

6. In Class Assessment

In the second block, students are introduced to a series of case studies that provide examples of how the law shapes aspects of the criminal justice system. For each case study, we locate and explore relevant law, critically examine how law facilitates the use of power and/or regulates decision-making, and familiarise ourselves with mechanisms available to ensure accountability, review, and/or appeal. Seminar topics include:

1. Police Powers and Responsibilities

2. Sentencing Law 

3. The Law of Corrections

4. Law and its Role in Youth Justice and Child Protection


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
